Test Description: Register default-value verification and masked write/read compliance across the address list defined for the GPIO block.
Remarks: Addresses with read_mask_array[i]==0 or write_mask_array[i]==0 are explicitly skipped; addresses flagged in skip_rst_array[]/skip_array[] are skipped.
Test Steps / Procedure:
Environment/Setup:
- Use data structures defined in test_define.c: addr_array[], default_value_array[], read_mask_array[], write_mask_array[], skip_rst_array[], skip_array[], and CNT (number of mapped registers).
Initialization:
- Clear failure counters: def_fail_cnt = 0, wr_fail_cnt = 0.
Reset/Default-value verification (in-order for i = 0..CNT-1):
- For each addr = addr_array[i]:
  - If skip_rst_array[i] == 1, skip this address.
  - If read_mask_array[i] == 0x00000000, skip reading.
  - Read data_rd = read_reg(addr); compute data = (data_rd & 0xFFFFFFFE).
  - Compare data against default_value_array[i]; on mismatch, increment def_fail_cnt and print the mismatch detail.
Write stimulus (six patterns, j = 0..5):
- Patterns applied in order: 0xFFFFFFFF, 0xAAAAAAAA, 0x55555555, 0xF5F5F5F5, 0xA5A5A5A5, 0xFFFF0000 (assigned to data_wr).
- For each pattern, iterate i = 0..CNT-1 and for each addr = addr_array[i]:
  - If skip_array[i] == 1, continue.
  - If write_mask_array[i] == 0x00000000, skip writing.
  - Write masked data: write_reg(addr, (data_wr & write_mask_array[i])).
Read/Verify (same iteration order i = 0..CNT-1):
- For each addr = addr_array[i]:
  - If skip_array[i] == 1, continue.
  - If write_mask_array[i] == 0x00000000 or read_mask_array[i] == 0x00000000, skip reading/verification.
  - Read data_rd = (read_reg(addr) & read_mask_array[i]).
  - Compute wr_n = (write_mask_array[i] ^ 0xFFFFFFFF).
  - Compute expected exp_val = ((data_wr & read_mask_array[i] & write_mask_array[i]) | (wr_n & read_mask_array[i] & default_value_array[i])).
  - If data_rd != exp_val, increment wr_fail_cnt and print the mismatch detail.
Cleanup/Termination:
- If (def_fail_cnt > 0) or (wr_fail_cnt > 0), finish(1); otherwise, finish(0).
Validation / Acceptance Criteria: PASS when both counters are zero at termination: def_fail_cnt == 0 and wr_fail_cnt == 0; any mismatch during default read or write-read comparison increments counters and results in finish(1).
Gap Analysis: No negative tests (e.g., invalid addresses) and no error-retry strategy; registers marked non-readable/non-writable are skipped without alternate verification.

Test Description: Negative-edge interrupt enable and handling for GPIO pads 8–39; verifies per-pin enable, group status set, per-pin/raw clear, and system IRQ clear.
Remarks: Conditional build selects GPIO0 (IRQ 87) or GPIO1 (IRQ 88) paths; per-pin config writes use bit positions 20, 18, and 16; uses int_pend as a run-loop gate with a timeout (5000).
Test Steps / Procedure:
Environment/Setup:
- Enable GIC interrupt: IRQ 87 if GPIO0 is defined; IRQ 88 if GPIO1 is defined.
- Enable system register interrupt output by writing MIZAR_LSS_SYSREG_INTR_EN1 with either LSS_SYSREG_INTR_EN1_GPIO0_INTR or LSS_SYSREG_INTR_EN1_GPIO1_INTR.
Initialization/Configuration:
- Drive all pads high: write 0xFFFFFFFF to address 0xA0243FFC.
- For each pin index i = 0..31 (pads 8..39):
  - Configure per-pin register at (MIZAR_GPIO_GP0_GPIO_8 + (i * 4)) with (1u<<20) | (1u<<18) | (1u<<16).
  - Wait 10 time units (wait_on(10)).
Per-pin stimulus and arming (loop i = 0..31):
- Compute wr_val = (1u << i).
- Clear raw status: write wr_val to MIZAR_GPIO_GPIO_INTR_RAW_STCLR1.
- Enable group interrupt for the pin: write wr_val to MIZAR_GPIO_GP0_INTR1_INTR_EN1; wait 10 time units.
- Arm ISR gate: set int_pend = 1.
- Generate a falling edge on the selected pin using 0xA0243FFC writes:
  - Write 0xFFFFFFFF (ensure high); wait 30 time units.
  - Write bitwise NOT of wr_val to 0xA0243FFC (drive only the selected bit low -> negedge).
Runtime handling (wait with timeout):
- Set timeout = 5000; while (int_pend == 1 and timeout > 0): wait 10 time units and decrement timeout.
- On timeout expiry: log error and increment test_err.
Interrupt service (Default_IRQHandler):
- Compute local_wr = (1u << i); clear run-loop gate: int_pend = 0.
- Restore pads high by writing 0xFFFFFFFF to 0xA0243FFC.
- Read per-pin register at (MIZAR_GPIO_GP0_GPIO_8 + (i * 4)) into rdata.
- If (rdata & 0x1) != 0, increment test_err.
- If (rdata & 0x2) != 0x0 then:
  - Read group status MIZAR_GPIO_GP0_INTR1_INTR_STS1 into rdata_grp; if ((rdata_grp & local_wr) == 0) increment test_err.
  - Reprogram per-pin register at (MIZAR_GPIO_GP0_GPIO_8 + (i * 4)) with (1u<<20) | (1u<<16).
  - Clear raw status: write local_wr to MIZAR_GPIO_GPIO_INTR_RAW_STCLR1.
  - Verify group status cleared: read MIZAR_GPIO_GP0_INTR1_INTR_STS1; if non-zero, increment test_err.
  - Clear system raw interrupt: write LSS_SYSREG_RAW_STCR1_GPIO0_INTR or LSS_SYSREG_RAW_STCR1_GPIO1_INTR to MIZAR_LSS_SYSREG_RAW_STCR1; then clear GIC (IRQ 87 or 88).
- Else (if (rdata & 0x2) == 0), increment test_err.
Cleanup/Termination:
- After iterating all pins, finish(test_err).
Impacted Registers: MIZAR_LSS_SYSREG_INTR_EN1, MIZAR_GPIO_GP0_GPIO_8, MIZAR_GPIO_GPIO_INTR_RAW_STCLR1, MIZAR_GPIO_GP0_INTR1_INTR_EN1, MIZAR_GPIO_GP0_INTR1_INTR_STS1, MIZAR_LSS_SYSREG_RAW_STCR1, 0xA0243FFC
Validation / Acceptance Criteria:
- No timeout events for any pin (i = 0..31).
- In ISR: (rdata & 0x2) must be non-zero; corresponding bit in MIZAR_GPIO_GP0_INTR1_INTR_STS1 must be set on entry and 0 after clears.
- System raw status in MIZAR_LSS_SYSREG_RAW_STCR1 is cleared; GIC interrupt is cleared.
- Overall: test_err remains 0 at finish().
Gap Analysis: No explicit negative tests (e.g., spurious or simultaneous interrupts); no debounce/noise robustness checks; relies on a global iteration index i inside ISR without guarding concurrent updates.

Test Description: Positive-edge interrupt enable across all pads 8–39; verifies group status set, raw-per-pin clear, sysreg clear, and re-enable for next iteration.
Remarks: Conditional build selects GPIO0 (IRQ 87) or GPIO1 (IRQ 88) paths; per-pin posedge enable uses 0x00020000; int_pend is volatile gate with a timeout (2000).
Test Steps / Procedure:
Environment/Setup:
- Enable GIC interrupt: IRQ 87 if GPIO0 is defined; IRQ 88 if GPIO1 is defined.
- Enable system register interrupt output by writing MIZAR_LSS_SYSREG_INTR_EN1 with either LSS_SYSREG_INTR_EN1_GPIO0_INTR or LSS_SYSREG_INTR_EN1_GPIO1_INTR.
Configuration:
- For each pin index i = 0..31 (pads 8..39): write 0x00020000 to (MIZAR_GPIO_GP0_GPIO_8 + (i * 4)) to enable posedge detection.
- Wait 10 time units.
- Program IO control groups: write 0x000000FF to each of MIZAR_GPIO_GPIO_IO_CTRL_GROUP1, GROUP2, GROUP3, GROUP4.
- Wait 10 time units.
- Enable group interrupt output: write 0xFFFFFFFF to MIZAR_GPIO_GP0_INTR1_INTR_EN1.
Stimulus per iteration (loop i = 0..31):
- Drive pads low: write 0x00000000 to 0xA0243FFC; wait 10 time units.
- Arm ISR gate: int_pend = 1.
- Generate rising edge: write 0xFFFFFFFF to 0xA0243FFC.
Runtime handling (wait with timeout):
- Set timeout = 2000; while (int_pend == 1 and --timeout > 0): wait 10 time units.
- On timeout expiry: log error, increment test_err, and break out of loop.
- Post-iteration: drive pads low again (0x00000000 to 0xA0243FFC) and wait 10 time units.
Interrupt service (Default_IRQHandler):
- Compute wr_val = (1u << i); clear run-loop gate: int_pend = 0.
- Read group status: rdata_grp = MIZAR_GPIO_GP0_INTR1_INTR_STS1; mask group during service by writing 0x00000000 to MIZAR_GPIO_GP0_INTR1_INTR_EN1.
- If (rdata_grp & 0xFFFFFFFF) != 0 then SUCCESS; else ERROR and increment test_err.
- Clear raw per-pin status for all pins: for j = 0..31 write 0x00010000 to (MIZAR_GPIO_GP0_GPIO_8 + (j * 4)); wait 2 time units.
- Verify group status cleared: read MIZAR_GPIO_GP0_INTR1_INTR_STS1; if non-zero, increment test_err.
- Clear system raw interrupt in MIZAR_LSS_SYSREG_RAW_STCR1 using LSS_SYSREG_RAW_STCR1_GPIO0_INTR or LSS_SYSREG_RAW_STCR1_GPIO1_INTR; read back MIZAR_LSS_SYSREG_RAW_STCR1 and if bit remains set, increment test_err.
- Re-enable group interrupt output: write 0xFFFFFFFF to MIZAR_GPIO_GP0_INTR1_INTR_EN1; clear GIC IRQ (87 or 88).
Cleanup/Termination:
- After iterating all pins, finish(test_err).
Impacted Registers: MIZAR_LSS_SYSREG_INTR_EN1, MIZAR_GPIO_GP0_GPIO_8, MIZAR_GPIO_GP0_INTR1_INTR_EN1, MIZAR_GPIO_GP0_INTR1_INTR_STS1, MIZAR_GPIO_GPIO_IO_CTRL_GROUP1, MIZAR_GPIO_GPIO_IO_CTRL_GROUP2, MIZAR_GPIO_GPIO_IO_CTRL_GROUP3, MIZAR_GPIO_GPIO_IO_CTRL_GROUP4, MIZAR_LSS_SYSREG_RAW_STCR1, 0xA0243FFC
Validation / Acceptance Criteria:
- No timeout events during any of the 32 iterations.
- Group interrupt status (MIZAR_GPIO_GP0_INTR1_INTR_STS1) is non-zero on entry to ISR and becomes 0 after raw clears.
- System raw status in MIZAR_LSS_SYSREG_RAW_STCR1 clears and remains cleared after readback; GIC interrupt is cleared.
- Overall: test_err equals 0 at finish().
Gap Analysis: No explicit negative tests (e.g., spurious triggers, simultaneous edges); raw clear is broadcast to all pins without per-pin isolation check; no debounce/noise robustness checks.
